summary: The 11 unique RDI-owned bootstrap constructor installs were enriched with .pdata ranges, direct/indirect call profiles, string literal references, and RDI-base slot use-sites. This turns the previous constructor-install lattice into a semantic worklist with three named anchors and several concrete dependency/consumer edges.

tool_update.change: pe-function-literals now accepts a single .pdata-resolved function address, matching pe-function-calls behavior. Constructor literal enrichment no longer requires a separate pe-runtime-functions pass to expand START..END ranges by hand.
tool_update.new_behavior: A function argument without ':' or '..' is normalized as VA/RVA and resolved through .pdata; unresolved addresses still warn and callers can pass an explicit START:END or START..END range.

call_profile_by_slot.note: Very small 0x13-byte constructor range; likely wrapper/trivial initializer until use-sites prove more.
Largest allocation in this worklist at 0x1894E8 bytes.

slot_use_site_summary.note: 0x140026E80 reads client+0x198D0 multiple times and also reads client+0x198E0, tying auth-controller and directlogin/varc candidates together.
0x140196F00 is the constructor installed at client+0x198C0, so client+0x198C0 construction depends on the earlier client+0x19940 object.
Only the generic teardown/reset helper at 0x14001C520 and the bootstrap install at 0x140021A40 were found with RDI-base scanning.
Only the generic teardown/reset helper at 0x14001C520 and the bootstrap install at 0x140021A40 were found with RDI-base scanning, despite ObjCache/PreloadList literals in the constructor.

relationship_to_prior_findings: Preserves the cleaned 11-slot RDI-owned constructor-install map.
Strengthens client+0x198D0 as the auth-controller anchor and links it to a nearby client+0x198E0 directlogin/varc candidate through shared consumer 0x140026E80.
Adds a concrete object-cache/preload-list anchor at client+0x199B8, which is likely relevant to JS5/object-definition preload behavior rather than login directly.
Adds a cross-slot dependency where client+0x198C0 construction reads the large client+0x19940 object.
opennxt_947_server_implications: Login/auth parity should inspect 0x140026E80 next because it consumes both the known auth controller and the newly named directlogin/varc candidate.
Object/cache preload parity should inspect client+0x199B8 and constructor 0x1401A9790 because it has explicit ObjCache and PreloadList labels.
The large client+0x19940 object is initialized before client+0x198C0 and is consumed by that constructor, so startup order matters for any server-facing subsystem reached through 0x198C0.
Slots with only bootstrap/teardown RDI references may still be used through copied child pointers or non-RDI owner registers; they are not dead, just not directly consumed through RDI in this pass.
next_targets: Map 0x140026E80 because it joins client+0x198D0 and client+0x198E0 and likely sits on the login/directlogin consumer path.
Run literal/call/field-ref enrichment on 0x1401A9790 children and 0x14067BF80/0x14067C1F0 to understand ObjCache/PreloadList semantics.
Inspect 0x140196F00 around the client+0x19940 read at 0x140197013 to type the dependency between the largest bootstrap object and client+0x198C0.
Consider adding a compact constructor-worklist enrichment exporter once one more worklist pass confirms this runtime/calls/literals/field-refs bundle is the right repeated abstraction.
